respectful.definition: Showing care and consideration for others.
respectful.example: It is respectful to listen when someone speaks.

mindful.definition: Being careful and aware of something.
mindful.example: Be mindful of how your child is feeling.

enterprise.definition: An activity or project that takes effort.
enterprise.example: Think of parenting as a shared enterprise.

habit.definition: Something you do regularly, often without thinking.
habit.example: Get into the habit of talking to your baby.

pause.definition: A short stop or break before continuing.
pause.example: Leave a pause after telling your child what will happen.

pushchair.definition: A small chair with wheels for pushing a young child around (stroller).
pushchair.example: The baby was sitting in the pushchair.

unbuckling.definition: Opening or undoing a buckle or strap.
unbuckling.example: Now I'm unbuckling you from the seat.

awkward.example: You may feel awkward talking to a baby who can't reply.

nappy.definition: A piece of cloth or material worn by a baby to catch waste (diaper).
nappy.example: Let me change your nappy now.

involve.definition: To include someone in an activity.
involve.example: Involve your child in daily activities.

sensitive.definition: Able to notice and understand small changes in feelings.
sensitive.example: Be sensitive to your child's body language.

subtleties.definition: Small differences that are not easy to notice.
subtleties.example: Pay attention to the subtleties of their gestures.

agitation.definition: A state of being nervous, upset, or restless.
agitation.example: Watch for signs of agitation in your baby.

relaxation.definition: The state of being calm and free from tension.
relaxation.example: Relaxation in a baby means they feel safe.

ward.definition: To keep something harmful away; to prevent.
ward.example: Paying attention can ward off unhappiness.

despair.example: A neglected child may fall into despair.

boring.example: Early parenting can sometimes feel long and boring.

meaning.definition: The purpose or importance of something.
meaning.example: Observing your child gives parenting more meaning.

positive.example: Positive attention is never wasted on your child.

wasted.definition: Used without getting any benefit; thrown away.
wasted.example: Time spent with your child is never wasted.

err.example: We can err in thinking big events matter most.

grand.example: It's not the grand gestures that matter most.

theme.example: A trip to a theme park is fun but not what matters most.

everyday.example: Everyday interactions matter more than special events.

interactions.definition: The ways people communicate and act with each other.
interactions.example: Small daily interactions build strong bonds.

minutiae.definition: Very small, detailed parts of something.
minutiae.example: The minutiae of daily life are what shape a child.

satisfying.definition: Giving a feeling of pleasure and fulfillment.
satisfying.example: Small moments can be the most satisfying.

tone.definition: The quality of someone's voice that shows their feelings.
tone.example: Notice the tone of voice when someone speaks.

speaker.example: Focus on the speaker, not on your own thoughts.

prematurely.definition: Too early; before the right time.
prematurely.example: Don't prematurely prepare your response while listening.

wandering.definition: Moving around without direction; losing focus.
wandering.example: Notice when your mind is wandering during conversation.

focusing.definition: Giving all your attention to one thing.
focusing.example: Focusing on the speaker is a skill you can practice.

equal.definition: The same in importance or status.
equal.example: Be an equal partner in the dialogue.

glance.example: Notice the subtleties of a glance.

gesture.definition: A movement of the body that expresses something.
gesture.example: A small gesture can say a lot.
